settings.title: Book a Road-Trip Check Before the Summer Getaway
settings.excerpt: A loaded car heading off in July puts strain on the exact parts a quick check would have caught in June.
settings.body:
Summer is when families pile four people and a fortnight of luggage into a car that has barely left town all year. It is also our busiest season for roadside breakdown calls, and the two facts are not a coincidence.

So we are running a flat-rate holiday check through June. We go over tyres including the spare, brakes, coolant, air conditioning, and battery health, and flag anything that would spoil a long drive. A car that copes fine with short school runs can struggle the moment it is fully loaded and held at motorway speed for hours.

What we check

  • Tyres and pressures, including the spare and any kit
  • Brakes, coolant level, and a battery health test
  • Air conditioning, washers, wipers, and all the lights

The two things that catch people out most are tyres and coolant. A tyre that looks fine in town can be down to the wire on the inner edge, and a slow coolant leak only shows itself once the engine works hard in the heat. Both are cheap to sort now and miserable to discover on a hard shoulder.

It takes about an hour and we will tell you straight whether the car is fit for the trip or needs a small job first. If something does turn up, you will hear what it is and what it costs before we touch it.

Book early in June if you can. The week before the schools break up is always our tightest, and a check is only useful if there is still time to fix whatever it finds.
